changes_made: Changed 'k8s_cluster_auto_scaling_enabled' to 'k8s_cluster_autoscaling_enabled' for consistency with common terminology
review_notes: The functions generally map well to the concept of system recovery and failover in Kubernetes. The naming conventions are mostly followed, but a minor adjustment was made for consistency. The functions reflect important aspects of Kubernetes' ability to handle failover and recovery scenarios.
naming_improvements: Ensure consistent use of 'autoscaling' as a single word to align with Kubernetes terminology
title: System Recovery And Reconstitution - System recovery and reconstitution | failover capability [withdrawn
step1_reasoning: The control CP-10(5) focuses on system recovery and reconstitution with failover capabilities. In Kubernetes, ensuring system recovery and failover can be addressed through several mechanisms. 'k8s_etcd_backup_configured' ensures that etcd, the key-value store for Kubernetes, has backups configured, which is crucial for recovery. 'k8s_apiserver_high_availability_configured' ensures that the API server, a critical component, is set up for high availability. 'k8s_cluster_auto_scaling_enabled' ensures that the cluster can automatically adjust its size to handle failover scenarios. 'k8s_node_failover_configured' ensures that node-level failover mechanisms are in place to maintain application availability.
